content.full_text:
MEMBERS

SENATOR WILLIAM A CRAVEN
VICE CHAIRMAN
SENATOR MARIAN BERGESON
SENATOR ROBERT BEVERLY
SENATOR DANIEL E BOATWRIGHT
SENATOR BILL GREENE
SENATOR BARRY KEENE
SENATOR BILL LOCKYER
SENATOR KENNETH L MADDY
SENATOR HERSCHEL ROSENTHAL
SENATOR ART TORRES

California Legislature

TIMOTHY A HODSON
STAFF DIRECTOR

DARREN P CHESIN
CONSULTANT

DEBORAH MITTEN
SECRETARY

Senate Committee
on

Elections and Reapportionment

STATE CAPITOL, ROOM 5081
SACRAMENTO, CA 95814
(916) 445-2601
FAX (916) 327-7229

SENATOR MILTON MARKS
CHAIR

December 8, 1992


Ms. Luisa Menchaca
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Sacramento, CA  95814

Dear Ms. Menchaca:

Earlier today, we discussed the question of an employee of the
state Senate who receives monetary compensation for services
rendered as an expert witness (e.g., research and testimony in
connection with a lawsuit or other legal action). You advised me
that, under the Political Reform Act and regulations of the Fair
Political Practices Commission, such compensation would not be
considered an honorarium. Rather, compensation earned as an
expert witness would be considered as earned income and thus
subject to all relevant statutes and regulations governing earned
income.

Please consider this as confirmation of our telephone
conversation earlier today.

Thank you for assistance in this request.

Sincerely,

TIMOTHY A. HODSON
Staff Director

TAH:dm

sections.question_synthetic: Whether monetary compensation received by a state Senate employee for services as an expert witness (research and testimony in legal proceedings) constitutes an honorarium under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Compensation earned as an expert witness is not considered an honorarium but rather earned income subject to all relevant statutes and regulations governing earned income under the Political Reform Act and FPPC regulations.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is incoming correspondence from Senate staff confirming FPPC advice given in a prior phone conversation, not a formal FPPC advice letter response. No formal question and conclusion sections present. The substance of FPPC's position is captured in the body of the letter but not in a structured Q&A format.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether monetary compensation received by a state Senate employee for services as an expert witness (research and testimony in legal proceedings) constitutes an honorarium under the Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: Compensation earned as an expert witness is not considered an honorarium but rather earned income subject to all relevant statutes and regulations governing earned income under the Political Reform Act and FPPC regulations.

You advised me that, under the Political Reform Act and regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission, such compensation would not be considered an honorarium. Rather, compensation earned as an expert witness would be considered as earned income and thus subject to all relevant statutes and regulations governing earned income. Please consider this as confirmation of our telephone conversation earlier today. Thank you for assistance in this request. Sincerely, TIMOTHY A. HODSON Staff Director TAH:dm
embedding.summary: This is a confirmation letter from Timothy A. Hodson (Staff Director of the California State Senate Committee on Television and Reapportionment) to FPPC staff member Luisa Menchaca documenting an earlier telephone conversation regarding the treatment of expert witness compensation for state Senate employees.
